title: Introduce kernel-level heatmap and cost-flow annotator for invocation traces
preview: Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to surface GPU/kernel-level queuing and contention hotspots correlated to token-level cost and per-stage latency. This PR adds a sampled, lightweight kernel-heatmap export and a cost-flow annotator that stitches stage timing with kernel occupancy to help SREs and runtime engine...
text: Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to surface GPU/kernel-level queuing and contention hotspots correlated to token-level cost and per-stage latency. This PR adds a sampled, lightweight kernel-heatmap export and a cost-flow annotator that stitches stage timing with kernel occupancy to help SREs and runtime engineers diagnose expensive invocation patterns. Design summary: 1) A new kernel heatmap sampler emits compact histograms of kernel queue wait and execution time per-device and per-invocation-phase; sampling is prioritized for requests that exceed a configurable latency or cost threshold. 2) A cost-flow annotator postprocesses traces to attach a per-invocation-stage cost attribution vector (model load, key-value cache miss penalties, kernel stalls, token-generation time) and synthesizes rollup markers used by dashboards. 3) Minimal on-path overhead: sampler uses atomic ring-buff...

title: per-tenant throughput SLA enforcer + shared burst token refactor
preview: Motivation: customers on Dedicated ask for predictable per-tenant throughput guarantees while still allowing short-term bursts across a shared fleet. This PR implements an in-runtime per-tenant throughput SLA enforcer plus a refactor of the fleet-wide shared burst token pool to support weighted allocation and backpr...
text:
Motivation: customers on Dedicated ask for predictable per-tenant throughput guarantees while still allowing short-term bursts across a shared fleet. This PR implements an in-runtime per-tenant throughput SLA enforcer plus a refactor of the fleet-wide shared burst token pool to support weighted allocation and backpressure coordination with the autoscaler.

Summary of changes: added a light-weight controller (runtime/fleet/sla_enforcer.go) that tracks per-tenant token budgets (tokens/sec) and allocates from the local host bucket first, then from the shared burst pool when available. The shared burst pool has been moved out of the legacy global dispatcher and into a refactored service (runtime/fleet/burst_pool.go) that exposes an async lease API and utilization metrics. The autoscaler now consumes burst-pool pressure signals to pre-warm capacity when pool exhaustion is sustained. Consol...

title: Eviction Balance Controller and Sampled Utilization Inspector for Dedicated Fleets
preview: Context: In several recent incidents we observed oscillatory tenant evictions driven by noisy instantaneous utilization samples from the admission controller. Motivation: reduce user-facing throttles and preserve committed throughput while still reclaiming capacity when needed. This PR introduces two coordinated com...
text: Context: In several recent incidents we observed oscillatory tenant evictions driven by noisy instantaneous utilization samples from the admission controller. Motivation: reduce user-facing throttles and preserve committed throughput while still reclaiming capacity when needed. This PR introduces two coordinated components: 1) Eviction Balance Controller (EBC) that applies a soft-decommit window with a configurable penalty curve and fairness drift limiter to spread eviction decisions across time and tenants, and 2) Sampled Utilization Inspector (SUI), a low-overhead agent-side sampler that feeds percentile-based utilization signals to the autoscaler instead of relying on single-sample probes. Key design points: EBC computes an eviction_score = (util_delta * penalty_multiplier(time_since_last_eviction) + backlog_factor) with a tunable sigmoid penalty curve to avoid immediate repeated e...

title: Add compacted invocation digests and anomaly briefs
preview: Context and motivation: We frequently see operator noise in long-running SLO investigations where individual invocation traces are large and repetitive. Responders spend time scrolling through kilobytes of token-level traces that differ only in a few fields. This PR introduces a compacted invocation digest format an...
text:
Context and motivation: We frequently see operator noise in long-running SLO investigations where individual invocation traces are large and repetitive. Responders spend time scrolling through kilobytes of token-level traces that differ only in a few fields. This PR introduces a compacted invocation digest format and a companion 'anomaly brief' summarizer to reduce noise, speed triage, and provide a stable summary for downstream alerting and runbooks.

What this changes: compacted invocation digests are a condensed trace representation persisted to our tracing backend for selected sampled invocations. Each digest encodes a small set of stable identifiers (callsite fingerprint, model_variant, tenant_id, prompt_shape_signature), coarse token-phase histograms (generation/encode/kv/memory), and a short anomaly vector (entropy_delta, cost_delta_pct, latency_tail_bucket). The anomaly brief ...

title: surgical-load-sentinel-and-transparent-readonly-fallback
preview: Context: We observed a pattern of transient KV backpressure spikes causing cascading retries and SLO violations during short regional pressure events. This change introduces a surgical load sentinel that detects short-lived KV pressure and routes affected requests to a transparent readonly fallback when safe. Motiva...
text: Context: We observed a pattern of transient KV backpressure spikes causing cascading retries and SLO violations during short regional pressure events. This change introduces a surgical load sentinel that detects short-lived KV pressure and routes affected requests to a transparent readonly fallback when safe. Motivation: Preserve tail latency and reduce amplified error/latency cascades without wholesale throttling of tenants. Keep changes conservative to avoid behavioral surprises. Design summary: 1) Load sentinel in controlplane monitors kvPressure, inflightPerTenant and recent error-rate histograms and computes a lightweight score. 2) When score exceeds a configured short window threshold the sentinel marks traffic for an incremental readonly fallback using a routing hint. 3) The readonly fallback path in route-mux preserves identity headers and KV snapshot tokens and returns best-e...

title: enable structured ci telemetry and lightweight presubmit profiles
preview: Motivation: presubmit runs have become a major contributor to CI latency and noisy alerts. We need two coordinated improvements: (1) standardized, structured telemetry across CI lanes so presubmit and merge pipelines emit consistent metrics; (2) a lightweight presubmit profile that runs fast, language-aware smoke ch...
text:
Motivation: presubmit runs have become a major contributor to CI latency and noisy alerts. We need two coordinated improvements: (1) standardized, structured telemetry across CI lanes so presubmit and merge pipelines emit consistent metrics; (2) a lightweight presubmit profile that runs fast, language-aware smoke checks on small diffs to speed feedback for engineers. This PR introduces a small telemetry bridge (OTLP -> Prometheus) and a new presubmit profile driver that selects minimal checks via a delta-based matcher.

What I changed (high level): condensed CI telemetry into a single structured schema (ci.build.*, ci.test.*, ci.job.duration_ms) and a small exporter in ci/telemetry that translates workflow annotations to OTLP and Prometheus-compatible metrics; refactored .github/workflows/presubmit.yml to add a new presubmit:light job that uses the delta router to select a compact set...

title: Add incident taxonomy dashboards + alerting (type/owner breakdowns + playbook links)
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds the initial Incident Taxonomy (v1) dashboards + a small set of alert rules, per the reliability program initiative. The goal is to make taxonomy classification measurable and to enable reporting/triage by incident_type + owner_team without exploding metric label cardinality.

Key deliverables...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds the initial Incident Taxonomy (v1) dashboards + a small set of alert rules, per the reliability program initiative. The goal is to make taxonomy classification measurable and to enable reporting/triage by incident_type + owner_team without exploding metric label cardinality.

Key deliverables in this PR:
- Grafana dashboard: **Incident Taxonomy (v1)**
 - incident volume and impact proxy panels (by tier, severity)
 - breakdowns by **incident_type** and **owner_team**
 - classification compliance (% unknown/unclassified)
 - MTTA/MTTR approximations using incident event timestamps
 - drilldowns from type -> owner -> recent incidents (table)
 - playbook links based on incident_type (panel links + table column)
- Prometheus rules:
 - recording rules to normalize/aggregate incident event counters into a stable set of labels
 - alert rules for (1) unclassified rate re...

title: Stratified burst budget distributor and tiered degradation plan for Dedicated fleet
preview: Motivation: Tenants on Dedicated fleets need more predictable degradation and fairer use of limited burst credits during transient demand spikes. Current global soft-burst pools provide throughput but cause noisy neighbor effects and opaque degradation patterns. This change introduces a stratified burst budget distr...
text:
Motivation: Tenants on Dedicated fleets need more predictable degradation and fairer use of limited burst credits during transient demand spikes. Current global soft-burst pools provide throughput but cause noisy neighbor effects and opaque degradation patterns. This change introduces a stratified burst budget distributor and a tiered degradation plan to give operators fine-grained control over how burst capacity is allocated and how tenants degrade when capacity is constrained.

What this PR does: Implements a new budget distributor that divides burst credits into named strata (critical, standard, opportunistic) and allocates credits to tenants based on configurable weights, recent utilization percentiles, and tenant commitment tiers. Adds an anticipatory refill algorithm (EWMA-based arrival estimate + percentile headroom multiplier) so short-lived surges are smoothed without inflati...

title: Introduce lightweight 'skillshot' CI lane and dep-lock gardener for devdep hygiene
preview: Context and motivation: Developers reported slow feedback for small iterative changes (15-25s setup + 3-7m test runs) which discourages frequent commits. This PR introduces a low-latency presubmit lane called 'skillshot' that exercises a narrow, language-aware subset of linters and smoke-tests to give sub-60s signal...
text:
Context and motivation: Developers reported slow feedback for small iterative changes (15-25s setup + 3-7m test runs) which discourages frequent commits. This PR introduces a low-latency presubmit lane called 'skillshot' that exercises a narrow, language-aware subset of linters and smoke-tests to give sub-60s signal for common quick changes. In parallel, it adds a 'dep-lock gardener' utility that detects stale devDependencies, suggests removals, and maintains a small curated allowlist for dev tooling. This is aimed at reducing CI noise, minimizing install time, and lowering presubmit flakiness.

What changed (high level):
- Add new GitHub Actions workflow .github/workflows/ci-skillshot.yml that runs a trimmed toolchain (node install cache, python venv warm, quick lint + unit smoke) and returns status quickly.
- Introduce a modular presubmit-adapter pattern in infra/ci/adapters/ to plu...

title: Packed INT8 microkernel rewrite and token coalescer to improve throughput for small-batch inference
preview: Context and motivation: small-batch interactive workloads (chat, per-request short-context generation) show poor INT8 utilization because microkernel packing and per-token scheduling produce many narrow GEMM shapes and high per-call overhead. Prior batching heuristics mitigated this at the request layer but left ine...
text:
Context and motivation: small-batch interactive workloads (chat, per-request short-context generation) show poor INT8 utilization because microkernel packing and per-token scheduling produce many narrow GEMM shapes and high per-call overhead. Prior batching heuristics mitigated this at the request layer but left inefficiencies inside the kernel and scheduling layer. This PR introduces two coordinated changes to address that: (1) a packed INT8 microkernel optimized for narrow micro-tiles and reduced memory traffic, and (2) a runtime token coalescer that merges short token sequences across concurrent requests into aligned micro-batches at the kernel boundary.

Design summary:
- Packed INT8 microkernel: new micro-tiling layout (packed 4x8 major order) that reduces memory stride and enables vector-friendly inner loops; fused requantization step merged into the GEMM write-back to avoid a f...

title: Drift‑resilient collective scheduler and KV slab consolidation to reduce NCCL contention
preview: Context and motivation: In production we continue to see sporadic tail latency spikes on multi‑GPU TP workloads correlated with short windows of NCCL congestion and many small KV cache allocations from continuous batching. This PR introduces two coordinated runtime changes: (1) a drift‑resilient collective scheduler...
text:
Context and motivation: In production we continue to see sporadic tail latency spikes on multi‑GPU TP workloads correlated with short windows of NCCL congestion and many small KV cache allocations from continuous batching. This PR introduces two coordinated runtime changes: (1) a drift‑resilient collective scheduler that perturbs and staggers NCCL launches to avoid pathological synchronization across tensor slices, and (2) a KV slab consolidation pass that reduces small allocation churn and improves L2/L3 locality for the KV cache allocator. The combined change targets multi‑GPU tensor‑parallel inference paths (TP runtime) and the continuous‑batching code path used by the API router.

What this PR does (high level): - Adds Scheduler::PerturbationWindow, a lightweight jittering layer that applies per‑peer launch offsets within a configurable window to break lockstep NCCL launches while...

title: introduce-policy-contracts-kms-commitment-ledger-and-residency-gate
preview: Context and motivation: Customers operating under jurisdictional controls asked for stronger, auditable guarantees that (1) requests that claim a region-bound residency are actually served with region-anchored CMKs, (2) operator principles cannot bypass tenant-scoped RBAC policies, and (3) audit trails contain verif...
text: Context and motivation: Customers operating under jurisdictional controls asked for stronger, auditable guarantees that (1) requests that claim a region-bound residency are actually served with region-anchored CMKs, (2) operator principles cannot bypass tenant-scoped RBAC policies, and (3) audit trails contain verifiable cryptographic markers tying requests to KMS commitments. This PR implements three coordinated pieces: (A) a lightweight Policy Contracts DSL and compiler that lets product and compliance own high-level contracts (e.g., "Tenant must use CMK in eu-west-1 for classification=restricted") and compiles into RBACv2 assertions and enforcer rules; (B) a KMS Commitment Ledger service that records authoritative, append-only KMS assignment decisions per request (signed by the enforcer) and surfaces a compact proof token that is attached to audit events; (C) a Residency Gate enfor...

title: Coordinated credential handoff, compact retry presets, and generator stream ergonomics across SDKs
preview: Motivation: We keep getting support tickets and cross-SDK bugs around: (1) credential handoff semantics when a streaming connection needs to rotate/refresh tokens mid-session, (2) divergent ergonomic surfaces for generator/async-iterator based streaming in each SDK, and (3) inconsistent retry presets that lead to su...
text:
Motivation: We keep getting support tickets and cross-SDK bugs around: (1) credential handoff semantics when a streaming connection needs to rotate/refresh tokens mid-session, (2) divergent ergonomic surfaces for generator/async-iterator based streaming in each SDK, and (3) inconsistent retry presets that lead to surprising latency/cost tradeoffs in low-latency apps. This PR centralizes the contract in the monorepo and ships coordinated changes for the TypeScript/Python/Go SDKs and runtime float so customers can opt into a single predictable behaviour.

What this PR does (high-level): Add a CredentialHandoff contract (lightweight interface + reference implementation), introduce a new compact retry preset set (compact-1/2/3) tuned for short-sequence, latency-sensitive workloads, and unify the public generator/async-iterator surface across SDKs so streaming ergonomics are consistent and...

title: consolidate-credential-prefetch-and-stream-api-hygiene
preview: Motivation: multiple SDKs had duplicated fragile logic around credential prefetch/lease warmup and slightly divergent streaming ergonomics which caused intermittent auth races and inconsistent telemetry labels across Python/TS/Go. This PR centralizes prefetch/backoff presets, tightens stream lifecycle semantics, and...
text: Motivation: multiple SDKs had duplicated fragile logic around credential prefetch/lease warmup and slightly divergent streaming ergonomics which caused intermittent auth races and inconsistent telemetry labels across Python/TS/Go. This PR centralizes prefetch/backoff presets, tightens stream lifecycle semantics, and harmonizes telemetry tags so multi-language integrations show coherent traces and cost attribution. Scope: 1) introduce a small cross-SDK contract (neutral JSON blob) that describes a credential prefetch profile (ttl_target_ms, warmup_window_ms, jitter_pct) and wire it into each SDK's credential manager; 2) surface a lightweight StreamHandle.close(reason) API in Python and TS and align Go CloseWithReason to match semantics (idempotent, synchronous drain then close); 3) add adaptive retry presets for auth failures (fast-fail under 401->refresh->retry) while preserving exist...

title: Add Optimization Config change permissions + operator roles (apply/promote/rollback)
preview: Summary: Introduces explicit RBAC permissions for the Optimization Config lifecycle (create/validate/apply/promote/rollback) and adds two opinionated roles used by Console + control plane workflows. This is part of the Safe apply + rollback mechanism project to ensure rollback is both auditable and access-controlled...
text: Summary: Introduces explicit RBAC permissions for the Optimization Config lifecycle (create/validate/apply/promote/rollback) and adds two opinionated roles used by Console + control plane workflows. This is part of the Safe apply + rollback mechanism project to ensure rollback is both auditable and access-controlled. Motivation: Today, optimization config changes are implicitly covered by broad “platform operator” capabilities in some environments, and not consistently modeled in Private deployments. Enterprise customers and Security review require least-privilege roles with clear separation between “can propose” and “can execute rollback in prod.” Changes included: 1) New permissions: optimization_config.read, optimization_config.create_version, optimization_config.validate, optimization_config.diff, optimization_config.apply, optimization_config.promote, optimization_config.rollback...

title: traffic burst shielding and graceful-degradation rollout guard
preview: Motivation: we see a class of incidents where sudden client-driven traffic bursts (spikes in request ingress slope) overwhelm KV-cache memory and batching heuristics, producing high tail latency and queueing even when nominal throughput is within capacity. Existing canary detectors focus on gradual regressions or st...
text:
Motivation: we see a class of incidents where sudden client-driven traffic bursts (spikes in request ingress slope) overwhelm KV-cache memory and batching heuristics, producing high tail latency and queueing even when nominal throughput is within capacity. Existing canary detectors focus on gradual regressions or static threshold crossings; they tend to either miss very short sharp bursts or trigger hard rollbacks that are disruptive for live rollouts.

This PR introduces 'Burst Shielding' — a new detector + graded mitigation rollout-guard that: 1) detects sudden traffic bursts using a fused signal of p99 latency delta, request ingress slope, request queue depth, and per-device KV cache eviction rate; 2) classifies burst severity with a Nyquist-inspired adaptive smoothing and causal-change detector to avoid false positives on regular traffic ramps; 3) applies graded, time-bound mitiga...

title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations
preview: Title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations. Context: As part of offline bundle signature verification, the installer now supports a policy-gated break-glass flag (see redwood-private-installer pr-24979). This PR adds cluster-side guardrails so that (1) break-glass cannot be ...
text: Title: Add break-glass verification override guardrails + required audit annotations. Context: As part of offline bundle signature verification, the installer now supports a policy-gated break-glass flag (see redwood-private-installer pr-24979). This PR adds cluster-side guardrails so that (1) break-glass cannot be enabled silently, (2) use is attributable and exportable via audit logs, and (3) only approved operators can authorize it. Summary of changes: (A) Adds a Gatekeeper ConstraintTemplate + Constraint enforcing required annotations whenever break-glass is enabled via the Redwood Private install metadata ConfigMap (redwood-private/install-metadata) or Helm values rendered into that ConfigMap. Required annotations: redwood.ai/break-glass-ticket (SUP-/INT-/customer ticket id), redwood.ai/break-glass-reason (freeform, min 20 chars), redwood.ai/break-glass-approved-by (corporate ema...

title: Introduce per-prompt temporal baseline probe, drift-aware CI gate, and lightweight counterfactual replayer
preview: Motivation: Some prompt-set regressions we observe are intermittent and tied to recent temporal shifts in training data or model weights. Existing cohort/anchor approaches flag regressions but often require manual baseline selection or suffer from noise when the underlying distribution evolves. This PR adds a Per-Pr...
text:
Motivation: Some prompt-set regressions we observe are intermittent and tied to recent temporal shifts in training data or model weights. Existing cohort/anchor approaches flag regressions but often require manual baseline selection or suffer from noise when the underlying distribution evolves. This PR adds a Per-Prompt Temporal Baseline (PTB) probe that maintains short- and long-window baselines per prompt, a drift-aware CI gate that reasons about recent baseline movement before failing a job, and a lightweight counterfactual replayer used to prioritize regressions for human review.

Design summary: The PTB probe (eval_harness.probes.ptb_probe) computes exponentially weighted aggregates (EWMA) of configured metrics per prompt across two windows: short (default 1d) and long (default 30d). The probe exposes three signals: absolute delta (short vs long), variance ratio, and a stability ...

title: Add trigger endpoint for Optimize quality gating (config diff + baseline)
preview: Context: Optimize needs a first-class way to run offline evals against a proposed serving config change (batching/caching/quantization profile) before allowing apply/rollout. This PR adds a new API endpoint and job plumbing in eval-harness to accept a config diff + baseline reference, execute the requested gate tier...
text: Context: Optimize needs a first-class way to run offline evals against a proposed serving config change (batching/caching/quantization profile) before allowing apply/rollout. This PR adds a new API endpoint and job plumbing in eval-harness to accept a config diff + baseline reference, execute the requested gate tier against selected prompt sets, and return structured metrics plus artifact pointers suitable for Console/Optimize consumption. Endpoint: POST /api/v1/gates/trigger (auth: service-to-service token; scoped to optimize-gating). Request (JSON): gate_tier ("smoke"|"full"), model_id, baseline (baseline_model_version + baseline_runtime_config_ref), proposed (runtime_config_diff, optional proposed_model_version), prompt_sets (ids), sample_count (optional; defaults per tier), run_metadata (route_id, account_id, region, commit_sha, idempotency_key). Validation: limits request payload...

title: Require incident_type/subtype on create; auto-resolve owner + escalation channel
preview:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program “Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping”, we’re making incident_type a required field at incident creation time, and we want the bot to resolve an owner team consistently (single primary owner) to reduce time-to-engage and improve reporting quality.

This PR updates the Sla...
text:
Context: As part of the Reliability Program “Incident taxonomy + ownership mapping”, we’re making incident_type a required field at incident creation time, and we want the bot to resolve an owner team consistently (single primary owner) to reduce time-to-engage and improve reporting quality.

This PR updates the Slack modal + slash command flow to require incident_type and incident_subtype (from the v1 allowlist), then performs owner resolution (type/subtype -> primary owner + escalation channel) and posts the result into the incident channel. The goal is: (1) classification is required and auditable, (2) owner is consistently derived from a versioned mapping, and (3) the IC gets an immediate “who owns this” + “where to escalate” suggestion.

User-facing changes:
- /incident create now blocks submission until incident_type is selected.
- incident_subtype is required when the type has ...

title: Policy engine: add priority classes and per-route budgets (deterministic decision logging + validation)
preview: Summary: This PR extends the policy engine to support (a) explicit priority classes (enterprise/standard/best-effort/internal) and (b) per-route budgets that can be consumed deterministically during admission control / overload protection. It also adds deterministic decision logging (stable reason codes + structured...
text: Summary: This PR extends the policy engine to support (a) explicit priority classes (enterprise/standard/best-effort/internal) and (b) per-route budgets that can be consumed deterministically during admission control / overload protection. It also adds deterministic decision logging (stable reason codes + structured fields) and a schema validation layer to keep policies auditable for enterprise escalations. Motivation: Hot-route capacity protection requires a single, explainable place to answer: "given tenant, route group, and current capacity signals, should we admit / throttle / shed / fallback". We need to do this with minimal per-request overhead and without introducing high-cardinality telemetry. This PR focuses on correctness + determinism; performance tuning (memoization/batched lookups) will follow. What’s in scope: 1) Policy schema additions: - priority_class: enum {INTERNAL,...

title: Slab concatenation + FlashMixer kernel with async KV coalescer and continuous-batching horizon
preview: Context: Operator-level profiling in the last quarter showed sustained stalls on small-to-medium sequence lengths due to scatter/gather overheads and suboptimal KV warmup for bursty traffic. This PR introduces a new attention strategy (SlabConcatenate + FlashMixer microkernel) that reduces per-token overhead for het...
text: Context: Operator-level profiling in the last quarter showed sustained stalls on small-to-medium sequence lengths due to scatter/gather overheads and suboptimal KV warmup for bursty traffic. This PR introduces a new attention strategy (SlabConcatenate + FlashMixer microkernel) that reduces per-token overhead for heterogeneous sequence distributions and a lightweight async-KV coalescer that amortizes KV cache population across micro-batches. Motivation: 1) Reduce small-batch latency and increase throughput at mid-sequence lengths (256-2048) where customer workloads spend most of their tokens. 2) Retain deterministic rotary/rope semantics while enabling a fused concatenate path that avoids extra memcpy for tiled KV layouts. 3) Improve continuous batching horizon so runtime can opportunistically stretch batching without violating latency SLOs. What this change does: - Introduces FlashMix...

title: Introduce proportional failure heatmap and context coverage CI hook
preview: Motivation: some regressions surface only in high-volume contexts or in narrow prompt cohorts; existing eval gates treat each failing example equally which underweights production impact. This PR introduces a proportional failure heatmap and a context-coverage metric that (1) weights failures by frequency and severi...
text: Motivation: some regressions surface only in high-volume contexts or in narrow prompt cohorts; existing eval gates treat each failing example equally which underweights production impact. This PR introduces a proportional failure heatmap and a context-coverage metric that (1) weights failures by frequency and severity, (2) surfaces top-left hotspots in a time-decayed heatmap, and (3) produces a single coverage score used by the CI hook. Design: we cluster prompts into contextual cohorts using the existing prompt fingerprinting + semantic shingle embedding. For each cohort we compute a weighted failure score = sum_{examples in cohort}(failure_weight * recency_decay * frequency_factor). Frequency comes from the sampled traffic histogram (or sample proxy during CI). Severity is derived from configured eval metrics (exact-match, BLEU drop, hallucination flag), mapped to a 1-5 weight. We r...

title: Implement prompt fingerprint ledger and replay-safe CI enforcer
preview: Motivation: Flaky eval failures at the prompt-set level continue to surface from subtle dataset composition drift (sampling, paraphrase swaps, and tokenization edge cases) rather than model regressions. This PR implements a compact on-disk ledger and a CI-side enforcer that (1) records stable fingerprints for prompt...
text:
Motivation: Flaky eval failures at the prompt-set level continue to surface from subtle dataset composition drift (sampling, paraphrase swaps, and tokenization edge cases) rather than model regressions. This PR implements a compact on-disk ledger and a CI-side enforcer that (1) records stable fingerprints for prompt-sets+seed+model-config, (2) compares new runs against the ledger to flag composition-driven variance, and (3) provides a replay-safe enforcement mode that permits human review and controlled backfills before a hard CI failure.

Design summary: The fingerprint is a deterministic, salted hash that combines prompt identifiers, normalized prompt text, canonical tokenization signature, run-seed, and a lightweight output sketch (sampled token-level checksums plus aggregated metrics). A new ledger store (local by default; support for S3/GCS) holds one canonical entry per evaluate...

title: Add KMS throttle budget + request backpressure to prevent KMS saturation under spikes
preview: Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope...
text:
Context: We’ve seen intermittent elevated 5xx correlated with upstream KMS throttling (SUP-19377) and a near-miss retry amplification event that required a hotfix (pr-18563). While the wrapper now has sane retry/jitter defaults and circuit breaking, we still had a failure mode where a large burst of decrypt/envelope ops (e.g. cache cold start + traffic spike) could saturate KMS and extend the incident blast radius.

This PR introduces an explicit "KMS throttle budget" and integrates KMS calls with the existing request admission/backpressure path so we shed/queue work earlier rather than stampeding KMS.

What’s included (high level): (1) Per-process token-bucket budget for KMS operations: A lightweight budget that is decremented on KMS requests and refilled at a configured rate. When depleted, KMS calls are delayed (bounded) or rejected with a typed error depending on caller policy. Th...

title: adaptive qk-kv fusion with prefetching and rotary interleave for long-context throughput
preview: Motivation: Long-context workloads (>=16k tokens) dominate several customer workloads. Existing FlashAttention and separate QK/KV kernels yield suboptimal memory traffic when serving long sequences with continuous batching and active KV caching. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel strategy that fuses QK compute an...
text: Motivation: Long-context workloads (>=16k tokens) dominate several customer workloads. Existing FlashAttention and separate QK/KV kernels yield suboptimal memory traffic when serving long sequences with continuous batching and active KV caching. This PR introduces an adaptive kernel strategy that fuses QK compute and KV accumulation paths where beneficial, plus a lightweight KV prefetcher and a rotary-interleave layout to reduce memory bandwidth and kernel launch overhead. Design: 1) New fused kernels (CUDA) that compute QK scores and simultaneously write/aggregate KV fragments for future decode steps. These kernels are selected when sequence-length, batch composition, and kv cache hit heuristics indicate net win. 2) Adaptive kernel dispatcher: runtime heuristics (histogram of seq lengths, batch fill factor, GPU occupancy) choose between FlashAttention variants, fused QK-KV path, or t...

title: Throughput Sentinel: elasticity anchoring and microburst insulation for Dedicated fleets
preview: Motivation: Dedicated fleet autoscalers at scale have repeatedly oscillated on short workload shifts and microbursts. Scale-up reactions are generally fine, but scale-down decisions occasionally evict capacity too aggressively and fail to reserve microburst headroom for tenants with bursty traffic, causing transient...
text:
Motivation: Dedicated fleet autoscalers at scale have repeatedly oscillated on short workload shifts and microbursts. Scale-up reactions are generally fine, but scale-down decisions occasionally evict capacity too aggressively and fail to reserve microburst headroom for tenants with bursty traffic, causing transient SLA slippage. This PR introduces a small, conservative control-plane component — the Throughput Sentinel — which provides an elasticity anchoring layer that biases autoscaler decisions using a ratcheted utilization view and a microburst insulation budget per tenant.

Design summary: The Throughput Sentinel is an opt-in controller that sits between the utilization sampler and the scale decision engine. Key concepts:
- Ratcheted utilization: maintain a short-term percentile (P90/P95 by config) utilization envelope per tenant/pool and never drop below the envelope for scale-d...

title: add-load-diffusion-prober-and-smooth-fallback-oracle
preview: Motivation: production rollouts occasionally experienced sharp, short-duration latency spikes when traffic shifts concentrated on a small set of worker shards. This PR introduces a traffic-aware Load Diffusion Prober and a Smooth Fallback Oracle to reduce false-positive rollbacks and provide graceful stall protectio...
text: Motivation: production rollouts occasionally experienced sharp, short-duration latency spikes when traffic shifts concentrated on a small set of worker shards. This PR introduces a traffic-aware Load Diffusion Prober and a Smooth Fallback Oracle to reduce false-positive rollbacks and provide graceful stall protection during bursty traffic. The Load Diffusion Prober continuously samples routing keys and estimates effective shard utilization and collision probability; when hotspots are detected it adjusts probe weights and issues soft-penalties instead of hard rollbacks. The Smooth Fallback Oracle consumes prober signals plus percentile-residual smoothing to decide fallback windows and maximum rollback budgets. Implementation: - lightweight prober module with opt-in sampling policy tied to rollout gates - reservoir-based diffusion estimator that adapts window size by observed burstiness...

title: ci: generate release graph and manifest validator for private-installer artifacts
preview: Motivation: CI for private installer releases lacked a machine-readable release graph and a manifest-level validator that ties signed artifacts (images, helm charts, tf modules) back to the release manifest. This PR adds a release-graph generator and a manifest-validator stage to the private-installer CI pipeline to...
text: Motivation: CI for private installer releases lacked a machine-readable release graph and a manifest-level validator that ties signed artifacts (images, helm charts, tf modules) back to the release manifest. This PR adds a release-graph generator and a manifest-validator stage to the private-installer CI pipeline to ensure provenance, enable safer upgrades/rollbacks, and block unsigned/unaligned manifests from publishing. Summary of changes: - new pipeline step 'build:release-graph' which produces a directed release graph (JSON) that maps artifacts -> helm chart values -> terraform module versions and includes cryptographic digests and SBOM references; graph is collated from build outputs and attestation metadata - new pipeline step 'validate:manifest' that consumes a release graph and enforces: cosign signature verification (keyless and KMS), SBOM presence, chart values checksum, and...

title: Introduce lightweight tracepoint sampler and batch-pressure microbench for scheduler visibility
preview: Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to correlate scheduler decisions, KV-cache hot prefixes, and continuous-batching pressure in production-like workloads. Existing NVTX and perfetto exports are useful but either too heavy or too coarse for iterative scheduler tuning. This PR introduces a lightweight tracepoint s...
text:
Motivation: We need a low-overhead way to correlate scheduler decisions, KV-cache hot prefixes, and continuous-batching pressure in production-like workloads. Existing NVTX and perfetto exports are useful but either too heavy or too coarse for iterative scheduler tuning. This PR introduces a lightweight tracepoint sampler plus a compact microbench harness that reproduces batch-pressure scenarios seen in customer workloads.

What this PR does: 1) Introduces a TracepointSampler module (C++): periodic, low-payload tracepoints emitted around scheduler windowing and key KV-cache touchpoints. Sampling is rate-limited and context-tagged so it is safe to enable in Dedicated/Private pockets for short experiments. 2) Adds a BatchPressureMicrobench: a small harness that simulates mixed arrival patterns (burst+poisson), tracks batch fill/evict cycles, and reports per-step metrics (queue depth, av...

title: Introduce jurisdiction-aware assertion proxy, KMS consent router, and audit enrichment shim
preview: Motivation: customers with mixed-residency fleets asked for a deterministic place to evaluate operator/service jurisdiction assertions so KMS usage and audit provenance can be routed safely without sprinkling logic across services. This PR introduces an assertion proxy (service: residency-assertor) that normalizes i...
text: Motivation: customers with mixed-residency fleets asked for a deterministic place to evaluate operator/service jurisdiction assertions so KMS usage and audit provenance can be routed safely without sprinkling logic across services. This PR introduces an assertion proxy (service: residency-assertor) that normalizes incoming claims, evaluates configured jurisdiction policies, and emits a compact proof token used by downstream KMS routing and audit enrichment. It also adds a KMS Consent Router to pick an appropriate CMK/region and a lightweight audit-enrichment shim that attaches pseudonymous residency and proof metadata to audit events. Implementation summary: 1) residency-assertor service (new binary) with a small policy DSL converted into OPA-compatible JSON; 2) kms-consent-router middleware integrated into the existing kms-proxy client path with a decision cache and TTL; 3) audit-enr...

title: heterogeneous-attention-fusion-with-staged-scheduler-and-continuous-batching-support
preview: Motivation: reduce p99 latency regression for mixed-sequence workloads and improve throughput for production chat and retrieval runtimes by selecting optimal attention kernels at runtime and enabling continuous batching without sacrificing KV cache hit stability. Changes: Introduces a heterogeneous attention selecti...
text: Motivation: reduce p99 latency regression for mixed-sequence workloads and improve throughput for production chat and retrieval runtimes by selecting optimal attention kernels at runtime and enabling continuous batching without sacrificing KV cache hit stability. Changes: Introduces a heterogeneous attention selection layer (HeteroAttn) that picks between a low-latency microkernel and a high-throughput Flash-like kernel based on sequence length heuristics and current queueing pressure; adds a staged scheduler that enforces a short-term reservation window for latency-sensitive requests and a batch-aggregation window for throughput requests; implements a cooperative KV cache eviction policy (staged LRU) that works with continuous batching to avoid repeated cache thrash during rollups; adds fused rotary/rope offset precomputation paths that avoid memcpy for common rotary patterns; integr...

title: introduce-auth-tactician-ttl-prefetch-and-pullstream-refactor
preview: Context: Cross-SDK follow-up to earlier session/lease work — introduces an "auth tactician" component that centralizes token TTL awareness and a small prefetch policy to avoid tail latency for streamed pull/push APIs. Motivation: customers saw intermittent ~200-400ms spikes when long-running streams triggered last-s...
text: Context: Cross-SDK follow-up to earlier session/lease work — introduces an "auth tactician" component that centralizes token TTL awareness and a small prefetch policy to avoid tail latency for streamed pull/push APIs. Motivation: customers saw intermittent ~200-400ms spikes when long-running streams triggered last-second token refreshes; retry/backoff logic masked this but increased request churn and observable noise. Goal: reduce tail latency by proactively warming credentials within a TTL window, provide a small, well-documented opt-in prefetch policy across Python/TS/Go SDKs, and clean up PullStream ergonomics so the language SDKs expose a common surface for cancellable/asynchronous consumption. What I changed: 1) auth/tactician: new minimal stateful component (Python: redwood_sdk.session.tactician, TS: auth/tactician.ts, Go: session/tactician.go) that tracks token TTLs, schedules ...

title: Tokenwise scale-lowering and ring-buffer collation for quantized kernels
preview: Summary: Adds a tokenwise scale-lowering compiler pass and a ring-buffer collation stage in the runtime to reduce per-token quant/dequant churn and to coalesce KV cache writes from continuous-batching. Motivation: some workloads with short prefixes and frequent small continuations were suffering from per-token scale...
text:
Summary: Adds a tokenwise scale-lowering compiler pass and a ring-buffer collation stage in the runtime to reduce per-token quant/dequant churn and to coalesce KV cache writes from continuous-batching. Motivation: some workloads with short prefixes and frequent small continuations were suffering from per-token scale conversions and tight kernel launches that prevented microbatch coalescing on INT8 and FP8 execution paths. This PR targets the hot path by lowering scale operations earlier and grouping adjacent KV writes into a ring-backed collator so the GPU-side kernel sees larger contiguous work items without changing model numerics.

What changed (high-level):
- New compiler pass runtime/quant/scale_lowering.cc that detects consecutive token-scale operations and emits a lowered tokenwise scale applied as a fused kernel input where safe. The pass is careful to preserve per-head scalin...

title: Ephemeral runbook cache and idempotent rollforward handler for safe post-incident triage
preview: Context and motivation: On high-noise incidents the incident-bot runbook lookups and remediation orchestration were adding 150-300ms per request and occasionally executing non-idempotent handlers twice when a race in the triage state machine retried a step. This PR introduces a bounded ephemeral runbook cache (in-me...
text: Context and motivation: On high-noise incidents the incident-bot runbook lookups and remediation orchestration were adding 150-300ms per request and occasionally executing non-idempotent handlers twice when a race in the triage state machine retried a step. This PR introduces a bounded ephemeral runbook cache (in-memory with TTL and stale-while-revalidate) to reduce lookup latency, and a 'rollforward' handler wrapper that enforces idempotency and safe retries for automated remediation actions. Goals: 1) reduce median oncall lookup latency under load, 2) prevent double-applies of non-idempotent remediation steps, 3) preserve existing runbook semantics and allow opt-out via annotation. Changes summary: - runbook cache: lightweight LRU with 1s default TTL, 5s stale-while-revalidate, metrics and per-team tunables - rollforward handler: decorator that records ephemeral execution tokens in ...

title: kv-prefetch-aware-batching-with-sparse-scratch-allocator
preview: Context: In production workloads with long chat histories the KV cache dominates transient GPU workspace allocations and forces arena growth during batching spikes. Motivation: Reduce peak GPU footprint while preserving latency SLOs and steady-state throughput. Summary: Introduces a KV-prefetch hotset buffer and a s...
text: Context: In production workloads with long chat histories the KV cache dominates transient GPU workspace allocations and forces arena growth during batching spikes. Motivation: Reduce peak GPU footprint while preserving latency SLOs and steady-state throughput. Summary: Introduces a KV-prefetch hotset buffer and a sparse scratch allocator to avoid inflating the global arena during continuous batching. The hotset prefetcher tracks request-level KV hotness and pre-populates a compact per-device hotset capable of serving the majority of cache hits without expanding the scratch arena. The sparse scratch allocator exposes a lightweight free-list for short-lived activation fragments and returns large contiguous ranges back to the arena quickly, reducing fragmentation. Key changes: 1) runtime/kv: prefetch_hotset component with heuristics for prefix/predictive prefetch and bounded memory cap....

title: introduce-tenant-kms-binding-enforcer-and-audit-linker
preview: Motivation: We need a stronger guardrail that prevents cross-tenant KMS usage in multi-tenant Dedicated/Private deployments while preserving operator ergonomics and auditability. This PR implements a runtime enforcer that ensures KMS key bindings are validated against the request's tenant/namespace claims before all...
text: Motivation: We need a stronger guardrail that prevents cross-tenant KMS usage in multi-tenant Dedicated/Private deployments while preserving operator ergonomics and auditability. This PR implements a runtime enforcer that ensures KMS key bindings are validated against the request's tenant/namespace claims before allowing encrypt/decrypt/sign operations. It also introduces an audit linker service that enriches existing audit events with KMS provenance (key id, provider, binding-check-result) and a correlation id that ties application requests to KMS calls for post-incident investigation. Design summary: (1) policy-operator plugin that evaluates declared kms.bindings for a tenant namespace and caches validated bindings in a short-lived policy store; (2) request-time gate in the kms-proxy that checks the caller's tenant claim against the policy store and denies calls that violate binding...

title: Add standardized overload error codes + JSON payload (Hosted API)
preview: Context: During overload we currently surface a mix of upstream timeouts, generic 503s, and ad-hoc JSON bodies depending on which hop rejects the request (gateway vs routing vs runtime admission). That makes client behavior inconsistent and increases retry storms (clients retry immediately on timeouts/unknown 5xx). ...
text:
Context: During overload we currently surface a mix of upstream timeouts, generic 503s, and ad-hoc JSON bodies depending on which hop rejects the request (gateway vs routing vs runtime admission). That makes client behavior inconsistent and increases retry storms (clients retry immediately on timeouts/unknown 5xx). This PR introduces a single canonical overload error response struct and a stable error-code taxonomy for Hosted API overload/load-shedding. Goal is to make overload failures explicit, machine-parseable, and consistent across the request path while remaining backwards compatible (additive fields only). This aligns with ADR-014 (Hosted overload error taxonomy) and sets up follow-on work in the gateway middleware + routing fallback (ENG-4823/ENG-4824).

What changed: 1) Add `OverloadErrorResponse` (shared API error type) with stable fields: `error.type=overload_error`, `error...

title: Introduce simultaneous prefetch stress scheduler and OOM spectra reporter
preview: Motivation: We observed correlated OOMs and long-tail latency spikes when many concurrent inferences attempted identical prefix prefetching/pinning at the same time. Small-scale reproductions masked the issue because they serialized prefetch. This PR introduces a synthetic scheduler that reproduces simultaneous pref...
text:
Motivation: We observed correlated OOMs and long-tail latency spikes when many concurrent inferences attempted identical prefix prefetching/pinning at the same time. Small-scale reproductions masked the issue because they serialized prefetch. This PR introduces a synthetic scheduler that reproduces simultaneous prefetch pressure at scale and a compact OOM 'spectra' reporter that summarizes allocation patterns leading to crashes.

What this change does: Adds a new workload runner (sim-prefetch-stressor) that can spawn thousands of virtual sessions with configurable prefetch windows, KV-cache priming patterns, and stagger jitter; the runner can optionally inject hold-until-commit semantics to simulate long-lived memory pinning. It also adds oom-spectra-reporter, a post-process tool that consumes core dumps/heap snapshots and produces an epochal histogram of allocation-site families, pea...

title: Add post-provision smoke canary for Dedicated pools
preview: Context: As part of the Provision-to-ready automation sprint, we want a workload-realistic validation step after a GPU node (or pool expansion) is marked provisioned but before we declare it Ready for inference workloads. The existing post-provision checks cover driver parity + NCCL smoke + kubelet join, but we were...
text:
Context: As part of the Provision-to-ready automation sprint, we want a workload-realistic validation step after a GPU node (or pool expansion) is marked provisioned but before we declare it Ready for inference workloads. The existing post-provision checks cover driver parity + NCCL smoke + kubelet join, but we were still missing a fast end-to-end “serving path” signal (runtime boot, model load, tokenization, request/streaming, basic latency sanity). This PR adds a lightweight post-provision smoke/perf canary for Dedicated pools. It runs as a short-lived Job in the target cluster/pool, executes a small fixed prompt set against the local inference endpoint (or local runtime shim in dev), and emits a single structured result that the provisioning pipeline can treat as a gate.

What this does: (1) Adds a new canary mode: post-provision/dedicated_smoke. (2) Provides a Kubernetes Job templ...

title: Record validation artifacts and failure reasons in Fleet Readiness service
preview: Context: Provision-to-ready automation needs actionable failure modes and durable artifacts (logs, summaries) per node before we can safely gate nodes from joining serving pools. Today the orchestrator/agent emits logs, but we lose structured per-step results once a node is recycled, and operators have to grep raw l...
text: Context: Provision-to-ready automation needs actionable failure modes and durable artifacts (logs, summaries) per node before we can safely gate nodes from joining serving pools. Today the orchestrator/agent emits logs, but we lose structured per-step results once a node is recycled, and operators have to grep raw logs to understand why a node is stuck in Validating. This PR adds persistence for validation step results and a structured failure reason field to the Fleet Readiness service, with an API to fetch the latest results for a node and an API-friendly summary intended for internal tooling (ops console/runbooks). Scope: store step name, start/end timestamps, duration_ms, status, failure_reason_code/message, and an artifact pointer (URI + checksum) to bundled logs uploaded by the validation agent. Also adds minimal telemetry for write/read success and payload sizes (no per-node hi...

title: stream-slice-attn-kernel-with-rope-interleaving-and-proactive-kv-warmup
preview:
Summary: Introduces a new stream-slice attention microkernel and scheduler integration that interleaves rope offsets at dispatch time and proactively warms KV cache for streaming/continuous-batching workloads.

Motivation: short, variable-length streams suffer high p99 first-token latency because attention kernels a...
text:
Summary: Introduces a new stream-slice attention microkernel and scheduler integration that interleaves rope offsets at dispatch time and proactively warms KV cache for streaming/continuous-batching workloads.

Motivation: short, variable-length streams suffer high p99 first-token latency because attention kernels and KV caches are optimized for batched, fixed-length inference. This PR adds a slice-oriented kernel that (1) fuses QK scaling, rope application, and numeric-stable softmax into a single path for short sequences, (2) interleaves rope/rope-offset computation to avoid re-applying rotary per-slice, and (3) exposes a proactive KV warmup API used by the continuous-batcher to prefetch likely KV ranges into the slab before tokens arrive.

Checklist:
- [x] implement stream-slice microkernel (CUDA + CPU fallback)
- [x] scheduler hook for proactive KV warmup and warmup window heurist...

title: Add Private minimum pack: versioned Grafana dashboard bundle (runtime + control plane + infra)
preview: Summary: This PR adds the first versioned Grafana dashboard bundle for the Private Observability Minimum Pack. The dashboards are designed to be shipped as immutable artifacts (checked into this repo) and later installed via `redwood-helm-charts`/`redwood-private-installer` (follow-ups). Scope: 1) Runtime overview d...
text: Summary: This PR adds the first versioned Grafana dashboard bundle for the Private Observability Minimum Pack. The dashboards are designed to be shipped as immutable artifacts (checked into this repo) and later installed via `redwood-helm-charts`/`redwood-private-installer` (follow-ups). Scope: 1) Runtime overview dashboard focused on golden signals + inference-specific panels (token throughput, batching, KV cache, GPU utilization). 2) Control plane health dashboard (gateway/auth/policy/config services) with standard service golden signals and dependency panels (DB/redis if present). 3) Infra dashboard for customer cluster basics required to triage most common Private incidents (node pressure, PVC pressure, pod restart loops, scrape status). Design notes / constraints: - Private environments require bounded label cardinality; dashboards avoid templating on high-cardinality labels like...

title: Introduce provisioning-rate-fader-and-surge-quench-for-smoother-burst-handling
preview: Motivation: We observed repeated microspikes in tenant traffic that triggered aggressive warm/pool provisioning which led to oscillation in headroom and higher cost. This PR implements a provisioning rate fader and a surge quench mechanism to smooth capacity onboarding for Dedicated fleets while preserving burst gua...
text:
Motivation: We observed repeated microspikes in tenant traffic that triggered aggressive warm/pool provisioning which led to oscillation in headroom and higher cost. This PR implements a provisioning rate fader and a surge quench mechanism to smooth capacity onboarding for Dedicated fleets while preserving burst guarantees.

What this PR does (summary): Introduces a rate-fading controller that caps the maximum provisioning throughput (nodes/min and GPUs/min) per tenant/fleet and gradually ramps allocations based on an observed warmup progress signal. Adds a surge_quench policy that temporarily reduces speculative warm preprovisioning when the system detects high churn or repeated short spikes to avoid thrash. Exposes new telemetry and control knobs for operators and a tenant-visible advisory metric for customers using Dedicated.

Key features: 1) provisioning_rate_fader: configurable ...

title: Add audit export permissions + default role mappings (enterprise admin vs auditor)
preview:
Summary
This updates the rbac-policy-bundle to include new permissions required for SIEM-ready audit export configuration + operational visibility. It also updates default role mappings to keep least-privilege separation between enterprise admins (manage exports) and auditors (read-only + status).

Context
The contr...
text:
Summary
This updates the rbac-policy-bundle to include new permissions required for SIEM-ready audit export configuration + operational visibility. It also updates default role mappings to keep least-privilege separation between enterprise admins (manage exports) and auditors (read-only + status).

Context
The control plane and Console are introducing audit export CRUD + delivery status endpoints behind a beta feature flag. This PR updates the central policy bundle so permissions are standardized across Hosted and Dedicated. Private/on-prem roles are intentionally unchanged for beta.

Linked work
- ENG-5173 (RBAC permissions for export management)
- Control plane endpoints: redwood/pr-28792 (RBAC) + redwood/pr-28766 (config API) (FYI)

What changed
1) New permission set (names + semantics)
- audit_exports.read
 - Allows viewing export configurations (destination type, enabled/disabled...

title: Integrate Smart Routing with burst admission decisions (fallback + backpressure)
preview: Motivation: Dedicated burst capacity requires routing to consult the Burst Admission Controller before borrowing from shared burst pools, and to behave safely when burst is denied (protecting non-burst tenants + preserving determinism). This PR wires the routing/gateway path into the admission controller, adds deter...
text: Motivation: Dedicated burst capacity requires routing to consult the Burst Admission Controller before borrowing from shared burst pools, and to behave safely when burst is denied (protecting non-burst tenants + preserving determinism). This PR wires the routing/gateway path into the admission controller, adds deterministic policy evaluation inputs, and defines the fallback/backpressure behaviors for denied/timeout cases. Summary of behavior: For Dedicated tenants with burst enabled, routing computes a BurstContext (tenant_id, region, hw_class, baseline allocation id, requested_concurrency/requested_tokens estimate, priority tier) and calls the Burst Admission Controller synchronously at request admission time. If admitted, routing tags the request with the admission grant_id and burst_pool_id and routes to the burst pool endpoint group; if denied, routing falls back to baseline dedic...

title: Add anchorwise fidelity audit and CI explainability hook
preview: Motivation: Teams running long-lived prompt sets have asked for more granular, explainable regression signals tied to 'anchors' (representative examples) so that CI failures are actionable without manual triage. This PR introduces an anchorwise fidelity audit that computes per-anchor fidelity and saliency deltas, su...
text:
Motivation: Teams running long-lived prompt sets have asked for more granular, explainable regression signals tied to 'anchors' (representative examples) so that CI failures are actionable without manual triage. This PR introduces an anchorwise fidelity audit that computes per-anchor fidelity and saliency deltas, surfaces interpretable failure reasons (e.g., token shift, hallucinated span, idempotency drop), and wires a CI hook that annotates PRs with a compact explainability report.

What this changes: adds a lightweight audit runner (hooks/anchorwise_audit.py) that: (1) replays the prompt-set against a reference baseline and the candidate change, (2) computes per-anchor metrics (fidelity_score, token_saliency_delta, semantic_overlap_pct), (3) runs a stacked heuristic classifier to attribute probable failure cause, and (4) emits a normalized JSON+CSV summary consumed by the CI hook. ...

title: Burst: standardize denial reason codes + surface to routing/gateway
preview: Motivation: During burst beta we’ve had multiple cases where customers/Support see generic 429/overload errors without actionable context (e.g., cap reached vs fleet safety vs cooldown). This PR standardizes a small, stable set of burst denial reason codes in the admission controller response and propagates them thr...
text: Motivation: During burst beta we’ve had multiple cases where customers/Support see generic 429/overload errors without actionable context (e.g., cap reached vs fleet safety vs cooldown). This PR standardizes a small, stable set of burst denial reason codes in the admission controller response and propagates them through routing -> gateway so we can (a) render clear customer-facing error messages, (b) expose denial breakdowns in Console and dashboards, and (c) reduce Support back-and-forth by making the denial cause inspectable. Summary of changes: 1) Introduce a versioned enum `BurstDenialReason` in `common/capacity` shared types with explicit semantics and non-overlapping categories. 2) Admission controller returns `decision=DENY` with `reason_code` and optional `retry_after_ms` for retryable denials (e.g., COOLDOWN_ACTIVE / POOL_SATURATED). 3) Routing stores the reason code in reque...

title: Propagate route + model version context into runtime cache metrics (with cardinality allowlists)
preview:
## Context
We’re landing the cache telemetry suite (hit/miss, reuse depth, evictions, wasted prefill). For the signals to be actionable in dashboards and in the recommendations MVP, we need consistent slicing keys that match how Redwood operates: route, model, model version, deployment mode, and region.

This PR wir...
text:
## Context
We’re landing the cache telemetry suite (hit/miss, reuse depth, evictions, wasted prefill). For the signals to be actionable in dashboards and in the recommendations MVP, we need consistent slicing keys that match how Redwood operates: route, model, model version, deployment mode, and region.

This PR wires request-scoped context from the gateway/control plane into the runtime metrics emitter and applies strict allowlists/coercion so we don’t accidentally create per-tenant/per-request label explosions.

Related: ENG-4823 (route/model context joinability), ENG-4821 (metric defs + tags).

## What this changes
- Adds `TelemetryContext` (derived from `RequestContext`) to runtime metric emission sites for cache metrics.
- Adds the following metric labels (when enabled):
 - `rw_route`: stable route identifier (normalized, allowlisted; NOT tenant-specific)
 - `rw_model`: model ide...

title: prioritized-rca-scoring-and-playbook-recommender
preview:
Motivation: on-call responders are overwhelmed by alert noise and often run a suite of irrelevant playbooks. This PR implements a lightweight root-cause scoring layer + playbook prioritizer that surfaces the most likely mitigations.

What it does (single-pass summary):
- Adds a signal fusion module that scores candi...
text:
Motivation: on-call responders are overwhelmed by alert noise and often run a suite of irrelevant playbooks. This PR implements a lightweight root-cause scoring layer + playbook prioritizer that surfaces the most likely mitigations.

What it does (single-pass summary):
- Adds a signal fusion module that scores candidate root causes from alert metadata, recent SLO burn-rate windows (via slo-toolkit hooks), and recent deploy/rollout activity.
- Introduces a playbook prioritizer that ranks runbooks by expected mitigation probability and time-to-mitigate (TTM).
- Adds a new /incident suggest-playbook command for Slack that returns 1-3 prioritized actions and confidence bands.
- Exposes an opt-in per-service configuration to tune weights and to pin preferred playbooks.
- Includes metrics and tracing for the scoring decisions to aid audits and regression debugging.

Why this is different fr...
